evals.prompt: Run a PESTLE analysis for a fintech startup considering market entry into the European Union. Time horizon: 0–18 months (near-term) and 18 months–3 years (medium-term).
evals.expected_output: A macro-environment.md artifact committed to the resolved layout path. The artifact names the scope (fintech, EU, both time horizons). Each of the six PESTLE dimensions — Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, Environmental — is assessed with at least one named, concrete signal (a specific law, a named economic indicator, a demographic trend, a named technology). A prioritization section identifies the top two or three forces by strategic materiality, each with a time horizon tag (near-term / medium-term) and a directional impact (tailwind / headwind / neutral). The framework (PESTLE) is named; no definition or verbatim framework text appears. The resolved path is surfaced before writing.
assertions: Scope is established first: geography (EU), industry (fintech), and time horizons (0–18 months, 18–36 months) are named
Each of the six dimensions has at least one named concrete signal — a specific regulation, rate, demographic trend, technology, or climate risk
No dimension is assessed with purely generic language ('technology is changing fast')
Prioritization section identifies the top two or three forces by strategic materiality with time horizon tag and directional impact
PESTLE is named as the framework; no definition, acronym expansion, or verbatim framework explanation appears in the artifact
The artifact is committed to the resolved path with frontmatter type: macro-environment; path is surfaced before writing
